task_completion.description: Open the ArmController solution in Visual Studio 2022.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual Studio 2022 is open, and the Solution Explorer pane clearly displays the 'ArmController' project.

task_completion.description: Correct the derivative calculation in PidController.cs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor shows PidController.cs with the derivative calculation corrected to use '(error - _previousError)' or equivalent standard discrete derivative logic, replacing the incorrect '(_previousError - error)'.

task_completion.description: Correct the clamping condition in JointLimiter.cs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor shows JointLimiter.cs with the clamping logic fixed so that it properly restricts movement past hard stops (e.g., returning the clamped limit when outside bounds, rather than the raw value).

task_completion.description: Correct the scaling math in VelocityScaler.cs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor shows VelocityScaler.cs with the scaling calculation changed to divide by 1000 (or multiply by 0.001) to correctly convert mrad/s to rad/s, replacing the incorrect multiplication by 1000.

task_completion.description: Build the ArmController project with zero errors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output window or Error List in Visual Studio shows a successful build message (e.g., 'Build: 1 succeeded') with 0 errors reported.

integrity.description: The agent used Visual Studio 2022 to edit and build the code as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio 2022 IDE interface is visible in the screenshots. The agent did not bypass the IDE by using a basic text editor (like Notepad) or exclusively using terminal commands to edit and build.

integrity.description: The agent edited the actual source files within the correct project directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file tabs in Visual Studio show the correct filenames (PidController.cs, JointLimiter.cs, VelocityScaler.cs) and the Solution Explorer reflects the ArmController project structure.

integrity.description: The build success message is from a genuine build action in Visual Studio.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Output window shows standard MSBuild/Visual Studio build logs with timestamps and standard formatting, not a manually typed or echoed string in a terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project path: C:\Users\Docker\source\repos\ArmController. Target files: PidController.cs, JointLimiter.cs, VelocityScaler.cs. Bug 1 (PidController.cs): Derivative term incorrectly uses '(_previousError - error)'; it must be changed to '(error - _previousError)'. Bug 2 (JointLimiter.cs): Clamping condition is inverted (returns raw value when outside limits); it must be fixed to return the clamped value. Bug 3 (VelocityScaler.cs): Incorrectly multiplies by 1000 to convert mrad/s to rad/s; it must be changed to divide by 1000.
